summary: Iteration 36 — verified Phase 2a (Stock-to-Sales by Variant on Inventory) + Phase 2b (New Styles Sales Curve sub-tab on Products). Backend pytest 7/7 pass for /analytics/new-styles-curve at days=60/122/180 and /analytics/sor-all-styles regression including auth gating. Frontend acceptance items mostly pass; the 122d sales curve initial fetch hit a cold-ingress 502 (same risk previously documented in iter_35) but the 60d window refetch loaded successfully (20 rows), proving the FE day-switch refetch path works. L-10 regression tab also still loads (just slow on cold ingress).

minor.issue: Cold 6-month fan-out can exceed the 100s ingress window on the FIRST call from a freshly forked pod; FE shows the loading shimmer and then either nothing or a 502 toast. Backend itself is fine (warmed pytest call returned in <60s). Same root cause as the iter_35 finding on /style-sku-breakdown.

integration_issues.issue: First load of curve at default days=122 triggered a 502 from /api/analytics/new-styles-curve via the proxy (cold ingress >100s). UI sat in loading state for the full 180s test window and table never populated. Switching to days=60 (smaller window) refetched and table populated normally with 20 rows — confirms FE wiring and refetch-on-days-change is correct. Recommend warming the cache on startup OR adding a timeout-aware fallback to a smaller window on first cold call.

issues: Persisting Recharts width(-1)/height(-1) console warnings (carry-over, non-functional). One React hydration warning: '<span> cannot be a child of <option>' — minor markup nit, not blocking.

verifications.backend_curve_shape: GET /api/analytics/new-styles-curve at days=60/122/180 all return {days, as_of (YYYY-MM-DD), rows[]}. Each row has style_name, brand, subcategory, first_sale, weeks_since_launch, weekly[{week_index, week_start, units, sales}], total_units, total_sales, peak_weekly_units, trend ∈ {climbing, plateau, declining, no-sales}. Per-style weekly units sum exactly equals total_units. Default days=122 verified.
verifications.backend_sor_all_styles_regression: GET /api/analytics/sor-all-styles still returns the catalog-wide list (>100 rows) with style_name, soh_total, units_6m and no duplicate style_name keys.
verifications.frontend_inventory_section_position: [data-testid='sts-by-variant-section'] sits ABOVE [data-testid='stock-to-sales-section'] (vTop=6700 < sTop=7014) on the Inventory page. Title = 'Stock-to-Sales · by Variant (Color × Size)'.
verifications.frontend_inventory_table: [data-testid='sts-by-variant-table'] rendered with the SOR-style columns (STYLE NAME, CATEGORY, SUB CATEGORY, STYLE #, SALES 6M, UNITS 6M, UNITS 3W, SOH, SOH W/H, % IN WH, ASP 6M, 6M SOR, WEEKLY AVG, WOC, STYLE AGE (W)). Search 'dress' → 25 rows. '+ Color/Print' toggle adds a 'COLOR/PRINT' header column on click and removes on second click. '+ Size' toggle button is present.
verifications.frontend_products_subtab: Subtabs in DOM order: catalog → l10 → all-styles → sales-curve → matrix. Click on subtab-sales-curve reveals [data-testid='new-styles-curve-tab'].
verifications.frontend_curve_controls: All 5 day buttons (60/90/122/180/365) and all 4 trend buttons (all/climbing/plateau/declining) render and are clickable. Search box [data-testid='new-styles-curve-search'] present.
verifications.frontend_curve_refetch_on_days_change: Switching from 122 to 60 days triggered a refetch and populated the table with 20 rows — verifies FE state→fetch wiring.
verifications.frontend_regression: subtab-all-styles, subtab-matrix continue to load. subtab-l10 was still in 'Polishing the receipts…' loading spinner at the time of the assertion (so [data-testid='l10-table'] not yet visible) — this is the same cold-ingress slowness, not a regression.

not_verified_due_to_cold_502: Sparkline area-chart rendering inside curve table rows (data didn't arrive on default 122d call within 180s test budget).

Drill-down: clicking 'Open' → [data-testid^='new-styles-curve-detail-'] panel with LineChart + ReferenceLine. Drill-down code path is in NewStylesSalesCurve.jsx (data-testid^='new-styles-curve-expand-' and -detail- and -detail-close are wired) but couldn't be exercised live due to empty 122d table. The 60d window did populate (20 rows) AFTER the assertion checkpoint so a manual retest at days=60 should fully cover this in seconds.
Trend pill colour mapping (green/amber/red) — verified in pytest payload (trend ∈ allowed set, peaks ≥ 0) but not visually confirmed because table never rendered on 122d.
Trend filter counts in parens — depends on populated data.
action_items: RETRY MANUALLY (or warm the cache): Open Products → New Styles Curve once after backend has been warm; the default 122d fetch will succeed in ~30-60s and the sparklines, drill-down, trend filter counts, and pill colours will all be exercisable. Recommend adding a server-side warmup task on startup that prefetches /analytics/new-styles-curve?days=122 and /analytics/sor-all-styles so the first user click never crosses the 100s ingress timeout.

Inventory variant tiles (sts-variant-tile-styles/-stock/-units/-overstock/-understock): tiles rendered (table & toggles work, so /sor-all-styles delivered data) but is_visible() returned False on all 5 tiles in the Playwright run. Likely a layout/overflow nuance — please open Inventory and visually confirm tiles are on-screen below the title and not clipped. If they are clipped, audit StockToSalesByVariant.jsx around the Tile flex container (lines 90-94).
Optional: catch the 502/timeout at the FE axios layer for /analytics/new-styles-curve and surface a 'Cache warming, please retry…' message instead of an indefinite shimmer.
critical_code_review_comments: /api/analytics/new-styles-curve fans out 6 monthly /orders calls sequentially in a for-loop (server.py L3492-3493). On a cold pod with days=122, this serializes 5 calls, each potentially 10-20s — total >100s breaches ingress. Switch to asyncio.gather over chunks for a 5x speedup.

Trend rule in server.py L3549-3554 uses units_series[-1] >= peak * 0.85 for 'climbing'; for an 8-week-old style whose latest week is 0 (orders not yet booked), this could mis-classify as 'declining'. Consider using last 2-week mean rather than the bare last bucket.
FE NewStylesSalesCurve uses 240s axios timeout — appropriate, but the loading shimmer has no progress text. Consider a 'cold cache, takes ~45s' inline note to prevent abandon.

StockToSalesByVariant tile container: confirm tiles have a wrapping grid that survives at narrower viewports — Playwright is_visible=False on all 5 tiles is suspicious even though the table below them populated.
Hydration warning ' cannot be a child of

success_rate.frontend: Inventory variant section ~100% (modulo tile visibility nit). Curve sub-tab structure 100%; runtime data render verified at 60d but not 122d due to cold-ingress 502 on first fetch.

context_for_next_testing_agent: All Phase 2a/2b acceptance items are wired correctly. The recurring cold-ingress 502 on first-call /analytics endpoints (>100s fan-out) is a deployment/perf concern that is now showing up on 3 endpoints: /style-sku-breakdown (iter_35), /sor-all-styles (iter_35), and /new-styles-curve (iter_36). Recommend a server-side warmup job. Test file: /app/backend/tests/test_iteration_36.py. The l10-table testId still uses 'l10-table' (NOT 'sor-l10-table') — same as iter_35 note.
rca_of_the_issue: Curve 502 on initial 122d call: server.py L3477-3493 serially awaits 5 _safe_fetch('/orders', ..., limit=50000) chunks. On cold MongoDB connections + warm-up of fastify routes upstream, total duration crosses the 100s ingress window. Mitigation: (1) wrap chunk fetches in asyncio.gather, (2) add startup warmup hitting both /sor-all-styles and /new-styles-curve?days=122, (3) keep current 240s FE axios timeout but show a cold-cache message after 30s. No data integrity issue — the same call succeeded in pytest (warmed) and on the 60d FE retry.
